# write-synapse-eval — Evaluation Criteria

**Skill:** write-synapse-eval
**Generated by:** write-synapse-eval self-eval (bootstrap)
**Version:** 1.0

> **EVAL scope.** Structural conformance + routing correctness + per-type mechanical correctness of the EVAL.md produced. EXCLUDES grading the *body quality* of the criteria emitted (that requires running gatekeeper against artifacts evaluated by EVAL.md outputs of this skill — out of scope here) and EXCLUDES grading the source artifact itself (that is `/synapse-gatekeeper`).

---

## Structural Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against the SKILL.md document and skill directory layout. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-S01:** SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with all required fields (`name`, `description`, `domain`, `intent`, `tags`, `user-invocable`, `argument-hint`)
- [ ] **EVAL-S02:** `domain: skill` and `intent: write` exist in `taxonomy/SKILL_TAXONOMY.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-S03:** Wrong-Tool Detection section exists and names specific sibling skills (`/improve-skill`, `/synapse-gatekeeper`, `/synapse-brainstorm`)
- [ ] **EVAL-S04:** SKILL.md is under 120 lines (router stays thin — slightly higher cap than synapse-creator's 100 because the existing-EVAL guard belongs at `[ROUTE]`)
- [ ] **EVAL-S05:** Every `Load:` path in SKILL.md resolves to a file in the skill directory
- [ ] **EVAL-S06:** Skill has a row in `registry/SKILL_REGISTRY.md` matching the path
- [ ] **EVAL-S07:** EVAL.md exists alongside SKILL.md
- [ ] **EVAL-S08:** All four `references/flow-<type>.md` files exist (skill, protocol, agent, tool)
- [ ] **EVAL-S09:** All four `templates/<type>/eval.md` files exist (skill, protocol, agent, tool)
- [ ] **EVAL-S10:** `references/type-config.md` exists and contains entries for all four types with `tier_prefixes`, `output_path_shape`, `output_filename`, `canonical_checklist_source`, `template_path`, and `flow_file` keys
- [ ] **EVAL-S11:** `references/shared-steps.md` exists and contains all four procedures (`validate-frontmatter`, `resolve-output-path`, `existing-eval-guard`, `write-eval-atomic`)
- [ ] **EVAL-S12:** `references/shared-steps.md` contains zero `if $TYPE` or `case $TYPE` conditionals (parameterization invariant)
- [ ] **EVAL-S13:** Domain README at `synapse/skills/skill/README.md` lists write-synapse-eval

---

## Execution Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against HOW the skill orchestrates execution. -->
<!-- Evaluated via execution trace inspection. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-E01:** `[ROUTE]` Hard Gate — `$TYPE` validated before any flow file loads
  - **Test:** With invalid `$TYPE` (e.g., `pathway`), trace shows failure and valid-list message at `[ROUTE]` BEFORE any `references/flow-*.md` is read.
  - **Fail signal:** A `flow-*.md` Read appears before `$TYPE` validation, OR the run proceeds with an invalid `$TYPE`.

- [ ] **EVAL-E02:** Single Flow Load — exactly one `flow-<type>.md` loaded per session
  - **Test:** Across an entire eval-generation session, count Read tool calls against `references/flow-*.md` paths. Must equal 1.
  - **Fail signal:** Count is 0 (no flow loaded) or > 1.

- [ ] **EVAL-E03:** Existing-EVAL guard — refuses to overwrite without `--force`
  - **Test:** Pre-place a non-empty file at the resolved `$EVAL_PATH`. Trace shows failure with redirect to `/improve-skill` BEFORE any flow body executes.
  - **Fail signal:** EVAL.md silently overwritten, OR redirect message missing.

- [ ] **EVAL-E04:** Atomic write — single Write call against `$EVAL_PATH`
  - **Test:** Inspect a successful run's trace. Exactly one Write tool call targets `$EVAL_PATH`. Zero writes touch the source artifact files.
  - **Fail signal:** Multiple writes against `$EVAL_PATH`, OR any write against the source `SKILL.md`/`TOOL.md`/agent/protocol `.md`.

- [ ] **EVAL-E05:** Wrong-Tool Redirect Honored — does not proceed when user intent mismatches
  - **Test:** When the user asks to grade or improve, trace shows redirect to `/synapse-gatekeeper` or `/improve-skill` and termination, NOT a `[ROUTE]` entry.
  - **Fail signal:** Skill proceeds to `[ROUTE]` after a wrong-tool match.

- [ ] **EVAL-E06:** Concurrency Contract — single artifact per invocation
  - **Test:** When the user asks for "evals for this skill and that protocol", trace shows a single-artifact rejection with parallel-agent guidance, NOT two flow loads.
  - **Fail signal:** Two flow files loaded in a single invocation.

- [ ] **EVAL-E07:** No-fallback invariant — transcription flows fail loud on missing checklist
  - **Test:** Temporarily rename `synapse/skills/skill/synapse-gatekeeper/references/protocol-checklist.md`; invoke `/write-synapse-eval protocol <path>`. Trace shows hard failure with the unresolved Load path, no EVAL.md written.
  - **Fail signal:** Skill proceeds to write an EVAL.md with criteria sourced from training memory.

- [ ] **EVAL-E08:** Bias-control invariant — `skill-eval-prompter` never sees `SKILL.md` body
  - **Test:** Inspect the parallel dispatch from `flow-skill:[D]`. Prompter's input contains only the skill name and `description`; never the SKILL.md body or `references/`.
  - **Fail signal:** Prompter dispatch carries SKILL.md content.

---

## Flow Conformance Criteria

<!-- Apply each independently to flow-skill.md, flow-protocol.md, flow-agent.md, flow-tool.md. -->
<!-- A criterion passes only if it passes for ALL four files. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-F01:** Each flow has a `[START]` node and a real `[END]` node
- [ ] **EVAL-F02:** Node headings use `### [ID]` (level-3) consistently
- [ ] **EVAL-F03:** Every node has Do, Don't, and Exit blocks (or explicit absence justified)
- [ ] **EVAL-F04:** Exit blocks declare labeled edges with conditions
- [ ] **EVAL-F05:** Per-node Loads — companion files load inside the node that uses them
- [ ] **EVAL-F06:** Registry/README writes do NOT happen in any flow (eval generation produces a single EVAL.md only)
- [ ] **EVAL-F07:** No `if $TYPE` or `case $TYPE` conditionals — flows are already type-specific by file path
- [ ] **EVAL-F08:** Flow file under 200 LOC for transcription flows; under 250 LOC for `flow-skill.md` (dispatch)
- [ ] **EVAL-F09:** Transcription flows (`flow-protocol.md`, `flow-agent.md`, `flow-tool.md`) name a canonical checklist Load path and assert hard-stop on Load failure
- [ ] **EVAL-F10:** Atomic-write invariant — every flow assembles in memory and calls `→ shared-steps:write-eval-atomic` exactly once

---

## Output Criteria — Per-Type Mechanical Correctness

### Test Prompt T1 — Skill EVAL generation

**Prompt:** `/write-synapse-eval skill synapse/skills/skill/synapse-brainstorm`

Acceptance checks:
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.1:** A new `EVAL.md` is written at `synapse/skills/skill/synapse-brainstorm/EVAL.md`
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.2:** EVAL.md contains an `EVAL-S` (Structural) section with at least one criterion
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.3:** EVAL.md contains an `EVAL-O` (Output) section sourced from `skill-eval-judge`
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.4:** EVAL.md contains a `Test Prompts` section with prompts grouped by persona
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.5:** EVAL.md does NOT contain inline criteria invented by the router (all `EVAL-O` come from agent dispatches)
- [ ] **EVAL-T1.6:** Source `SKILL.md` is unmodified (read-only invariant)

### Test Prompt T2 — Protocol EVAL generation

**Prompt:** `/write-synapse-eval protocol src/protocols/<domain>/<protocol>.md`

Acceptance checks:
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.1:** A new `<protocol>.eval.md` is written adjacent to the protocol file
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.2:** EVAL contains `EVAL-Sxx` criteria — one per row of `protocol-checklist.md` Tier 1
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.3:** EVAL contains `EVAL-Cxx` criteria — one per row of `protocol-checklist.md` Tier 2
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.4:** Pass-condition text is preserved verbatim from the source checklist
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.5:** No `Test Prompts` section appears
- [ ] **EVAL-T2.6:** Source protocol `.md` is unmodified

### Test Prompt T3 — Agent EVAL generation

**Prompt:** `/write-synapse-eval agent src/agents/<domain>/<agent>.md`

Acceptance checks:
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.1:** A new `<agent>.eval.md` is written adjacent to the agent file
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.2:** EVAL contains `EVAL-Sxx` criteria — one per row of `agent-checklist.md` Tier 1
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.3:** EVAL contains `EVAL-Qxx` criteria — one per row of `agent-checklist.md` Tier 2
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.4:** Pass-condition text is preserved verbatim from the source checklist
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.5:** No `Test Prompts` section appears
- [ ] **EVAL-T3.6:** Source agent `.md` is unmodified

### Test Prompt T4 — Tool EVAL generation

**Prompt:** `/write-synapse-eval tool src/tools/<domain>/<tool>/`

Acceptance checks:
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.1:** A new `EVAL.md` is written inside the tool directory
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.2:** EVAL contains `EVAL-Sxx` criteria sourced from gatekeeper Tool-flow rows
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.3:** EVAL contains one `EVAL-Xxx` per file in the tool's `test/` directory
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.4:** Each `EVAL-Xxx` cites a real test file path that exists on disk
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.5:** If `test/` is empty/missing, EVAL still writes (with empty EVAL-X tier) and a warning is surfaced
- [ ] **EVAL-T4.6:** Source `TOOL.md` and test files are unmodified

---

## Out of Scope (downstream)

The following criteria are explicitly NOT evaluated here:

- **Quality of the criteria written into the produced EVAL.md** — that is judged when the EVAL.md is consumed by `/synapse-gatekeeper` or `/improve-skill` against a real artifact.
- **Source artifact promotion readiness** — `/synapse-gatekeeper`'s job.
- **Score ≥ 80** for the source artifact — `/improve-skill` runs against the source.

write-synapse-eval is accountable for producing a mechanically correct EVAL.md that matches the canonical checklist (transcription flows) or the agent dispatch contract (skill flow). Judgment about whether those criteria are *good enough* lives downstream.
